metadata.title: States PCS: Manipur GK Questions for Manipur Public Service Commission Exams

questions.question: Which dynasty ruled Manipur prior to its status as a princely state under British India?
questions.explanation: The Meitei kingdom, also known as the Kingdom of Kangleipak, was the ruling power in Manipur before British colonization in the 19th century.

questions.question: Which queen of Manipur is famed for leading the resistance against the British during the Anglo-Manipur War of 1891?
questions.explanation: Queen Linthoingambi, wife of King Ningthoukhomba of Manipur, is renowned for her resistance during invasions, notably disguising herself as king to repel raiders. She is a legendary figure in Manipuri history and folklore, with her exploits chronicled in the Ningthourol Lambuba.

questions.question: Which freedom fighter from Manipur is known for his involvement with the Indian National Army (INA) alongside Subhas Chandra Bose?
questions.explanation: Colonel Shaukat Malik of the INA hoisted the national flag at Moirang, Manipur in 1944; Tikendrajit Singh opposed the British earlier, but INA’s key Manipur link is Malik at Moirang.

questions.question: Which World War II battle between INA and British forces took place in Manipur in 1944?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Imphal occurred in Manipur in 1944, marking a decisive halt to the Japanese and INA advance into India.

questions.question: Which script was traditionally used to write the Meitei language in Manipur before the adoption of the Bengali script for official purposes?
questions.explanation: Meitei Mayek is the indigenous script of Manipur, replaced by Bengali script during colonial rule and now revived for cultural preservation.

questions.question: Which British official was killed in Manipur during the 1891 rebellion, prompting a severe colonial crackdown?
questions.explanation: Chief Commissioner James Wallace Quinton was killed by Manipuri rebels in 1891, leading to British military intervention and execution of local leaders.

questions.question: Which classical dance form, originating in the royal courts of Manipur, is closely associated with Vaishnavite traditions?
questions.explanation: Raslila developed in Manipuri royal courts after the adoption of Vaishnavism and is based on Krishna’s life.

questions.question: In which year did Manipur officially merge with the Indian Union after independence?
questions.explanation: Manipur merged with India on 21 September 1949 after the Instrument of Accession was signed by the Maharaja.

questions.question: Which is the largest freshwater lake in Manipur, also considered the largest in Northeast India?
questions.explanation: Loktak Lake, in Manipur’s Bishnupur district, is Northeast India’s largest freshwater lake; it features phumdis and houses Keibul Lamjao National Park, home to the rare sangai deer.

questions.question: Which mountain range forms the boundary between Manipur and Myanmar?
questions.explanation: The Patkai Range forms Manipur’s eastern boundary with Myanmar and is part of the Purvanchal system.
